**Huttig** is a city in [Union County](/wiki/Union_County,_Arkansas "Union County, Arkansas"), [Arkansas](/wiki/Arkansas "Arkansas"), United States. As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), Huttig had a population of 448.[[3]](#cite_note-Census2020PLLede-3)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Huttig,_Arkansas&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city has a total area of 3.1 square miles (8.0 km2), of which 3.0 square miles (7.8 km2) is land and 0.1 square miles (0.26 km2) (3.25%) is water.

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Huttig,_Arkansas&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[8]](#cite_note-GR2-8) of 2000, there were 731 people, 282 households, and 197 families residing in the city. The population density was 245.4 inhabitants per square mile (94.7/km2). There were 321 housing units at an average density of 107.8 per square mile (41.6/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 48.70% [White](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), 48.43% [Black](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)") or [African American](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), 0.82% [Native American](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), 0.14% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), 0.82% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 1.09% from two or more races. 1.50% of the population were [Hispanic](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)") of any race.

There were 282 households, out of which 32.6% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 50.4% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 16.7% had a female householder with no husband present, and 29.8% were non-families. 28.0% of all households were made up of individuals, and 12.4% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.59 and the average family size was 3.22.

In the city, the population was spread out, with 25.9% under the age of 18, 8.9% from 18 to 24, 29.4% from 25 to 44, 22.7% from 45 to 64, and 13.1% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 36 years. For every 100 females, there were 96.0 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 88.9 males.

The median income for a household in the city was $25,284, and the median income for a family was $32,000. Males had a median income of $31,375 versus $18,056 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the city was $12,215. About 16.1% of families and 16.2% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 23.1% of those under age 18 and 11.3% of those age 65 or over.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Huttig,_Arkansas&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Education")]

Public education for elementary and secondary school students is provided by the [Strong–Huttig School District](/wiki/Strong%E2%80%93Huttig_School_District "Strong–Huttig School District"), which leads to graduation from [Strong High School](/wiki/Strong_High_School "Strong High School").

It was served by [Huttig School District](/wiki/Huttig_School_District "Huttig School District"), which operated Huttig Elementary School and [Huttig High School](/w/index.php?title=Huttig_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Huttig High School (page does not exist)").[[9]](#cite_note-9) On July 1, 2004, it consolidated with the [Strong School District](/wiki/Strong_School_District "Strong School District") to form the [Strong-Huttig School District](/wiki/Strong-Huttig_School_District "Strong-Huttig School District").[[10]](#cite_note-Schoolmerge-10)

* [Daisy Bates](/wiki/Daisy_Bates_(civil_rights_activist) "Daisy Bates (civil rights activist)")—civil rights activist
* [Floyd Cramer](/wiki/Floyd_Cramer "Floyd Cramer")—American Hall of Fame pianist
